With the guidance of the confidence map, the position of human head in estimated density map gets more attention to encode the final density map, which can avoid enormous misjudgements effectively. The crowd count can be obtained by integrating the final density map. To encode a highly refined density map, the total crowd count of each image is classified in a designed classification task and we first explicitly map the prior of the population-level category to feature maps. To verify the efficiency of our proposed method, extensive experiments are conducted on three highly challenging datasets. Results establish the superiority of our method over many state-of-the-art methods. 由于场景复杂性和规模变化，人群计数是一个具有挑战性的问题。尽管深度学习在人群计数方面取得了很大的进步，但场景的复杂性影响了这些方法的判断，他们通常会错误地将某些物体视为人；造成人群计数结果潜在的巨大错误。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新颖的端到端模型，称为群体注意力卷积神经网络（CAT-CNN）。我们的 CAT-CNN 可以通过自动编码置信度图来自适应地评估人头在每个像素位置的重要性。在置信图的指导下，估计密度图中人体头部的位置得到更多关注来编码最终的密度图，可以有效避免巨大的误判。人群数量可以通过整合最终的密度图来获得。为了编码高度精炼的密度图，在设计的分类任务中对每个图像的总人群计数进行分类，我们首先将人口级别类别的先验显式映射到特征图。为了验证我们提出的方法的效率，在三个极具挑战性的数据集上进行了广泛的实验。结果证明我们的方法优于许多最先进的方法。 本文提出了一种名为CAT-CNN的端到端模型，用于解决人群计数中因场景复杂性和尺度变化导致的误判问题。 Jiwei Chen,Wen Su,Zengfu Wang cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Xiaofeng Wang,Zheng Zhu,Fangbo Qin,Yun Ye,Guan Huang,Xu Chi,Yijia He,Xingang Wang
title: MVSTER: Epipolar Transformer for Efficient Multi-View Stereo
title_zh: MVSTER：用于高效多视图立体的极极变压器
abstract: Learning-based Multi-View Stereo (MVS) methods warp source images into the reference camera frustum to form 3D volumes, which are fused as a cost volume to be regularized by subsequent networks. The fusing step plays a vital role in bridging 2D semantics and 3D spatial associations. However, previous methods utilize extra networks to learn 2D information as fusing cues, underusing 3D spatial correlations and bringing additional computation costs. Therefore, we present MVSTER, which leverages the proposed epipolar Transformer to learn both 2D semantics and 3D spatial associations efficiently. Specifically, the epipolar Transformer utilizes a detachable monocular depth estimator to enhance 2D semantics and uses cross-attention to construct data-dependent 3D associations along epipolar line. Additionally, MVSTER is built in a cascade structure, where entropy-regularized optimal transport is leveraged to propagate finer depth estimations in each stage. Extensive experiments show MVSTER achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction performance with significantly higher efficiency: Compared with MVSNet and CasMVSNet, our MVSTER achieves 34% and 14% relative improvements on the DTU benchmark, with 80% and 51% relative reductions in running time. MVSTER also ranks first on Tanks&Temples-Advanced among all published works. Code is released at https://github.com/JeffWang987.
summary: Learning-based Multi-View Stereo (MVS) methods warp source images into the reference camera frustum to form 3D volumes, which are fused as a cost volume to be regularized by subsequent networks. The fusing step plays a vital role in bridging 2D semantics and 3D spatial associations. However, previous methods utilize extra networks to learn 2D information as fusing cues, underusing 3D spatial correlations and bringing additional computation costs. Therefore, we present MVSTER, which leverages the proposed epipolar Transformer to learn both 2D semantics and 3D spatial associations efficiently. Specifically, the epipolar Transformer utilizes a detachable monocular depth estimator to enhance 2D semantics and uses cross-attention to construct data-dependent 3D associations along epipolar line. Additionally, MVSTER is built in a cascade structure, where entropy-regularized optimal transport is leveraged to propagate finer depth estimations in each stage. Extensive experiments show MVSTER achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction performance with significantly higher efficiency: Compared with MVSNet and CasMVSNet, our MVSTER achieves 34% and 14% relative improvements on the DTU benchmark, with 80% and 51% relative reductions in running time. MVSTER also ranks first on Tanks&Temples-Advanced among all published works. Code is released at https://github.com/JeffWang987.
abstract_zh: 基于学习的多视图立体 (MVS) 方法将源图像扭曲到参考相机视锥体中以形成 3D 体积，这些体积被融合为成本体积，由后续网络进行正则化。融合步骤在桥接 2D 语义和 3D 空间关联方面发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，以前的方法利用额外的网络来学习 2D 信息作为融合线索，未充分利用 3D 空间相关性并带来额外的计算成本。因此，我们提出了 MVSTER，它利用所提出的极线 Transformer 来有效地学习 2D 语义和 3D 空间关联。具体来说，极线 Transformer 利用可拆卸的单目深度估计器来增强 2D 语义，并使用交叉注意力沿着极线构建依赖于数据的 3D 关联。此外，MVSTER 采用级联结构构建，利用熵正则化最优传输在每个阶段传播更精细的深度估计。大量实验表明，MVSTER 以显着更高的效率实现了最先进的重建性能：与 MVSNet 和 CasMVSNet 相比，我们的 MVSTER 在 DTU 基准上实现了 34% 和 14% 的相对改进，运行时间相对减少了 80% 和 51%。 MVSTER还在Tanks&Temples-Advanced所有已发表作品中排名第一。代码发布于 https://github.com/JeffWang987。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为MVSTER的多视角立体视觉方法，利用新的epipolar Transformer结构来提高重建效率和性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的多视角立体视觉方法在融合2D语义和3D空间关联时，依赖额外的网络进行学习，导致计算成本高且未充分利用3D空间相关性。
AI.method: MVSTER通过epipolar Transformer结构，结合可分离的单目深度估计器和跨注意力机制，同时学习2D语义和3D空间关联，并采用级联结构和熵正则化最优传输方法优化深度估计。
AI.result: MVSTER在DTU基准测试中相比MVSNet和CasMVSNet分别提升了34%和14%的性能，同时运行时间减少了80%和51%。此外，MVSTER在Tanks&Temples-Advanced数据集上排名第一。
AI.conclusion: MVSTER通过高效的epipolar Transformer结构和优化的级联设计，实现了多视角立体视觉任务中领先的重建性能和计算效率。

authors_text: Mohammad Reza Hosseinzadeh Taher,Fatemeh Haghighi,Michael B. Gotway,Jianming Liang
title: CAiD: Context-Aware Instance Discrimination for Self-supervised Learning in Medical Imaging
title_zh: CAiD：医学成像中自监督学习的上下文感知实例辨别
abstract: Recently, self-supervised instance discrimination methods have achieved significant success in learning visual representations from unlabeled photographic images. However, given the marked differences between photographic and medical images, the efficacy of instance-based objectives, focusing on learning the most discriminative global features in the image (i.e., wheels in bicycle), remains unknown in medical imaging. Our preliminary analysis showed that high global similarity of medical images in terms of anatomy hampers instance discrimination methods for capturing a set of distinct features, negatively impacting their performance on medical downstream tasks. To alleviate this limitation, we have developed a simple yet effective self-supervised framework, called Context-Aware instance Discrimination (CAiD). CAiD aims to improve instance discrimination learning by providing finer and more discriminative information encoded from a diverse local context of unlabeled medical images. We conduct a systematic analysis to investigate the utility of the learned features from a three-pronged perspective: (i) generalizability and transferability, (ii) separability in the embedding space, and (iii) reusability. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that CAiD (1) enriches representations learned from existing instance discrimination methods; (2) delivers more discriminative features by adequately capturing finer contextual information from individual medial images; and (3) improves reusability of low/mid-level features compared to standard instance discriminative methods. As open science, all codes and pre-trained models are available on our GitHub page: https://github.com/JLiangLab/CAiD.
summary: Recently, self-supervised instance discrimination methods have achieved significant success in learning visual representations from unlabeled photographic images. However, given the marked differences between photographic and medical images, the efficacy of instance-based objectives, focusing on learning the most discriminative global features in the image (i.e., wheels in bicycle), remains unknown in medical imaging. Our preliminary analysis showed that high global similarity of medical images in terms of anatomy hampers instance discrimination methods for capturing a set of distinct features, negatively impacting their performance on medical downstream tasks. To alleviate this limitation, we have developed a simple yet effective self-supervised framework, called Context-Aware instance Discrimination (CAiD). CAiD aims to improve instance discrimination learning by providing finer and more discriminative information encoded from a diverse local context of unlabeled medical images. We conduct a systematic analysis to investigate the utility of the learned features from a three-pronged perspective: (i) generalizability and transferability, (ii) separability in the embedding space, and (iii) reusability. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that CAiD (1) enriches representations learned from existing instance discrimination methods; (2) delivers more discriminative features by adequately capturing finer contextual information from individual medial images; and (3) improves reusability of low/mid-level features compared to standard instance discriminative methods. As open science, all codes and pre-trained models are available on our GitHub page: https://github.com/JLiangLab/CAiD.
abstract_zh: 最近，自监督实例辨别方法在从未标记的摄影图像中学习视觉表示方面取得了巨大的成功。然而，考虑到摄影图像和医学图像之间的显着差异，基于实例的目标（专注于学习图像中最具辨别力的全局特征（即自行车的车轮））的功效在医学成像中仍然未知。我们的初步分析表明，医学图像在解剖学方面的高度全局相似性阻碍了捕获一组不同特征的实例辨别方法，从而对其在医学下游任务中的性能产生负面影响。为了缓解这一限制，我们开发了一个简单而有效的自我监督框架，称为上下文感知实例歧视（CAiD）。 CAiD 旨在通过提供从未标记医学图像的不同本地上下文编码的更精细、更具辨别力的信息来改进实例辨别学习。我们进行了系统分析，从三个角度研究学习特征的效用：（i）泛化性和可转移性，（ii）嵌入空间的可分离性，以及（iii）可重用性。我们广泛的实验表明，CAiD (1) 丰富了从现有实例辨别方法中学到的表示； （2）通过从单个医学图像中充分捕获更精细的上下文信息来提供更具辨别力的特征； (3) 与标准实例判别方法相比，提高了低/中级特征的可重用性。作为开放科学，所有代码和预训练模型都可以在我们的 GitHub 页面上找到：https://github.com/JLiangLab/CAiD。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CAiD的自监督学习框架，通过利用医学图像的局部上下文信息来改进实例判别学习，以解决医学图像中解剖结构相似性导致的特征区分度不足问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基于实例判别的自监督方法在医学图像上效果不佳，因为医学图像的解剖结构相似性导致全局特征难以区分，影响下游任务性能。
AI.method: 开发了CAiD框架，通过编码医学图像的多样化局部上下文信息，提供更细粒度和判别性的特征，从而提升实例判别能力。
AI.result: 实验表明CAiD增强了现有方法的表示能力，通过捕捉个体医学图像的细微上下文信息获得更判别性的特征，并提升了低/中层特征的可复用性。
AI.conclusion: CAiD通过利用局部上下文信息有效缓解了医学图像解剖相似性带来的挑战，为医学影像的自监督学习提供了更优解决方案。

We conduct a systematic analysis to investigate the utility of the learned features from a three-pronged perspective: (i) generalizability and transferability, (ii) separability in the embedding space, and (iii) reusability. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that CAiD (1) enriches representations learned from existing instance discrimination methods; (2) delivers more discriminative features by adequately capturing finer contextual information from individual medial images; and (3) improves reusability of low/mid-level features compared to standard instance discriminative methods. As open science, all codes and pre-trained models are available on our GitHub page: https://github.com/JLiangLab/CAiD. 最近，自监督实例辨别方法在从未标记的摄影图像中学习视觉表示方面取得了巨大的成功。然而，考虑到摄影图像和医学图像之间的显着差异，基于实例的目标（专注于学习图像中最具辨别力的全局特征（即自行车的车轮））的功效在医学成像中仍然未知。我们的初步分析表明，医学图像在解剖学方面的高度全局相似性阻碍了捕获一组不同特征的实例辨别方法，从而对其在医学下游任务中的性能产生负面影响。为了缓解这一限制，我们开发了一个简单而有效的自我监督框架，称为上下文感知实例歧视（CAiD）。 CAiD 旨在通过提供从未标记医学图像的不同本地上下文编码的更精细、更具辨别力的信息来改进实例辨别学习。我们进行了系统分析，从三个角度研究学习特征的效用：（i）泛化性和可转移性，（ii）嵌入空间的可分离性，以及（iii）可重用性。我们广泛的实验表明，CAiD (1) 丰富了从现有实例辨别方法中学到的表示； （2）通过从单个医学图像中充分捕获更精细的上下文信息来提供更具辨别力的特征； (3) 与标准实例判别方法相比，提高了低/中级特征的可重用性。作为开放科学，所有代码和预训练模型都可以在我们的 GitHub 页面上找到：https://github.com/JLiangLab/CAiD。 本文提出了一种名为CAiD的自监督学习框架，通过利用医学图像的局部上下文信息来改进实例判别学习，以解决医学图像中解剖结构相似性导致的特征区分度不足问题。 Mohammad Reza Hosseinzadeh Taher,Fatemeh Haghighi,Michael B. Gotway,Jianming Liang eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Jinsheng Wang,Yinchao Ma,Shaofei Huang,Tianrui Hui,Fei Wang,Chen Qian,Tianzhu Zhang
title: A Keypoint-based Global Association Network for Lane Detection
title_zh: 基于关键点的车道检测全局关联网络
abstract: Lane detection is a challenging task that requires predicting complex topology shapes of lane lines and distinguishing different types of lanes simultaneously. Earlier works follow a top-down roadmap to regress predefined anchors into various shapes of lane lines, which lacks enough flexibility to fit complex shapes of lanes due to the fixed anchor shapes. Lately, some works propose to formulate lane detection as a keypoint estimation problem to describe the shapes of lane lines more flexibly and gradually group adjacent keypoints belonging to the same lane line in a point-by-point manner, which is inefficient and time-consuming during postprocessing. In this paper, we propose a Global Association Network (GANet) to formulate the lane detection problem from a new perspective, where each keypoint is directly regressed to the starting point of the lane line instead of point-by-point extension. Concretely, the association of keypoints to their belonged lane line is conducted by predicting their offsets to the corresponding starting points of lanes globally without dependence on each other, which could be done in parallel to greatly improve efficiency. In addition, we further propose a Lane-aware Feature Aggregator (LFA), which adaptively captures the local correlations between adjacent keypoints to supplement local information to the global association. Extensive experiments on two popular lane detection benchmarks show that our method outperforms previous methods with F1 score of 79.63% on CULane and 97.71% on Tusimple dataset with high FPS. The code will be released at https://github.com/Wolfwjs/GANet.
summary: Lane detection is a challenging task that requires predicting complex topology shapes of lane lines and distinguishing different types of lanes simultaneously. Earlier works follow a top-down roadmap to regress predefined anchors into various shapes of lane lines, which lacks enough flexibility to fit complex shapes of lanes due to the fixed anchor shapes. Lately, some works propose to formulate lane detection as a keypoint estimation problem to describe the shapes of lane lines more flexibly and gradually group adjacent keypoints belonging to the same lane line in a point-by-point manner, which is inefficient and time-consuming during postprocessing. In this paper, we propose a Global Association Network (GANet) to formulate the lane detection problem from a new perspective, where each keypoint is directly regressed to the starting point of the lane line instead of point-by-point extension. Concretely, the association of keypoints to their belonged lane line is conducted by predicting their offsets to the corresponding starting points of lanes globally without dependence on each other, which could be done in parallel to greatly improve efficiency. In addition, we further propose a Lane-aware Feature Aggregator (LFA), which adaptively captures the local correlations between adjacent keypoints to supplement local information to the global association. Extensive experiments on two popular lane detection benchmarks show that our method outperforms previous methods with F1 score of 79.63% on CULane and 97.71% on Tusimple dataset with high FPS. The code will be released at https://github.com/Wolfwjs/GANet.
abstract_zh: 车道检测是一项具有挑战性的任务，需要预测车道线的复杂拓扑形状并同时区分不同类型的车道。早期的工作遵循自上而下的路线图，将预定义的锚点回归为各种形状的车道线，由于固定的锚点形状，缺乏足够的灵活性来适应复杂的车道形状。最近，一些工作提出将车道检测制定为关键点估计问题，以更灵活地描述车道线的形状，并以逐点的方式逐渐将属于同一车道线的相邻关键点分组，这在后处理过程中效率低且耗时。在本文中，我们提出了一种全局关联网络（GANet），从一个新的角度来制定车道检测问题，其中每个关键点直接回归到车道线的起点，而不是逐点延伸。具体来说，关键点与其所属车道线的关联是通过全局预测其相对于相应车道起点的偏移量来进行的，彼此之间不依赖，可以并行完成，从而大大提高效率。此外，我们进一步提出了车道感知特征聚合器（LFA），它自适应地捕获相邻关键点之间的局部相关性，以将局部信息补充到全局关联中。对两个流行车道检测基准的大量实验表明，我们的方法优于以前的方法，在 CULane 上的 F1 分数为 79.63%，在高 FPS 的 Tusimple 数据集上的 F1 分数为 97.71%。代码将在 https://github.com/Wolfwjs/GANet 发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于关键点的全局关联网络（GANet），用于解决车道线检测中复杂形状建模和高效关联的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统锚点回归方法因固定形状缺乏灵活性，而关键点估计方法需逐点关联导致效率低下，需设计更高效的全局关联机制。
AI.method: 通过直接回归关键点到车道线起点的偏移量实现全局并行关联，并引入车道感知特征聚合模块（LFA）增强局部特征关联。
AI.result: 在CULane和Tusimple数据集上分别达到79.63%和97.71%的F1分数，且保持高FPS性能。
AI.conclusion: GANet通过全局关联与局部特征增强相结合，在车道线检测任务中实现了更高的准确率与效率。

authors_text: Tong Yang,Yifei Wang,Long Sha,Jan Engelbrecht,Pengyu Hong
title: Knowledgebra: An Algebraic Learning Framework for Knowledge Graph
title_zh: Knowledgebra：知识图的代数学习框架
abstract: Knowledge graph (KG) representation learning aims to encode entities and relations into dense continuous vector spaces such that knowledge contained in a dataset could be consistently represented. Dense embeddings trained from KG datasets benefit a variety of downstream tasks such as KG completion and link prediction. However, existing KG embedding methods fell short to provide a systematic solution for the global consistency of knowledge representation. We developed a mathematical language for KG based on an observation of their inherent algebraic structure, which we termed as Knowledgebra. By analyzing five distinct algebraic properties, we proved that the semigroup is the most reasonable algebraic structure for the relation embedding of a general knowledge graph. We implemented an instantiation model, SemE, using simple matrix semigroups, which exhibits state-of-the-art performance on standard datasets. Moreover, we proposed a regularization-based method to integrate chain-like logic rules derived from human knowledge into embedding training, which further demonstrates the power of the developed language. As far as we know, by applying abstract algebra in statistical learning, this work develops the first formal language for general knowledge graphs, and also sheds light on the problem of neural-symbolic integration from an algebraic perspective.
summary: Knowledge graph (KG) representation learning aims to encode entities and relations into dense continuous vector spaces such that knowledge contained in a dataset could be consistently represented. Dense embeddings trained from KG datasets benefit a variety of downstream tasks such as KG completion and link prediction. However, existing KG embedding methods fell short to provide a systematic solution for the global consistency of knowledge representation. We developed a mathematical language for KG based on an observation of their inherent algebraic structure, which we termed as Knowledgebra. By analyzing five distinct algebraic properties, we proved that the semigroup is the most reasonable algebraic structure for the relation embedding of a general knowledge graph. We implemented an instantiation model, SemE, using simple matrix semigroups, which exhibits state-of-the-art performance on standard datasets. Moreover, we proposed a regularization-based method to integrate chain-like logic rules derived from human knowledge into embedding training, which further demonstrates the power of the developed language. As far as we know, by applying abstract algebra in statistical learning, this work develops the first formal language for general knowledge graphs, and also sheds light on the problem of neural-symbolic integration from an algebraic perspective.
abstract_zh: 知识图（KG）表示学习旨在将实体和关系编码到密集的连续向量空间中，以便可以一致地表示数据集中包含的知识。从知识图谱数据集训练的密集嵌入有利于各种下游任务，例如知识图谱补全和链接预测。然而，现有的知识图谱嵌入方法不足以为知识表示的全局一致性提供系统的解决方案。我们基于对知识图谱固有代数结构的观察，开发了一种数学语言，我们将其称为 Knowledgebra。通过分析五个不同的代数性质，我们证明半群是通用知识图关系嵌入最合理的代数结构。我们使用简单的矩阵半群实现了实例化模型 SemE，该模型在标准数据集上展示了最先进的性能。此外，我们提出了一种基于正则化的方法，将源自人类知识的链式逻辑规则集成到嵌入训练中，这进一步证明了所开发语言的强大功能。据我们所知，通过将抽象代数应用于统计学习，这项工作开发了第一个通用知识图的形式语言，并从代数的角度阐明了神经符号集成问题。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Knowledgebra框架，通过抽象代数结构改进知识图谱的表示学习，解决现有方法在全局一致性上的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有知识图谱嵌入方法难以保证知识表示的全局一致性，缺乏系统性代数结构支持。
AI.method: 基于知识图谱的代数特性分析，确立半群为关系嵌入的最优结构，实现SemE模型并引入正则化方法整合逻辑规则。
AI.result: SemE在标准数据集上达到SOTA性能，验证了代数语言的有效性，并首次形式化构建通用知识图谱的代数框架。
AI.conclusion: 本工作通过抽象代数为知识图谱建立首个形式化语言，为神经符号系统融合提供了代数视角的解决方案。

authors_text: Takuya Mieno,Mitsuru Funakoshi
title: Data structures for computing unique palindromes in static and non-static strings
title_zh: 用于计算静态和非静态字符串中唯一回文的数据结构
abstract: A palindromic substring $T[i.. j]$ of a string $T$ is said to be a shortest unique palindromic substring (SUPS) in $T$ for an interval $[p, q]$ if $T[i.. j]$ is a shortest palindromic substring such that $T[i.. j]$ occurs only once in $T$, and $[i, j]$ contains $[p, q]$. The SUPS problem is, given a string $T$ of length $n$, to construct a data structure that can compute all the SUPSs for any given query interval. It is known that any SUPS query can be answered in $O(α)$ time after $O(n)$-time preprocessing, where $α$ is the number of SUPSs to output [Inoue et al., 2018]. In this paper, we first show that $α$ is at most $4$, and the upper bound is tight. We also show that the total sum of lengths of minimal unique palindromic substrings of string $T$, which is strongly related to SUPSs, is $O(n)$. Then, we present the first $O(n)$-bits data structures that can answer any SUPS query in constant time. Also, we present an algorithm to solve the SUPS problem for a sliding window that can answer any query in $O(\log\log W)$ time and update data structures in amortized $O(\logσ+ \log\log W)$ time, where $W$ is the size of the window, and $σ$ is the alphabet size. Furthermore, we consider the SUPS problem in the after-edit model and present an efficient algorithm. Namely, we present an algorithm that uses $O(n)$ time for preprocessing and answers any $k$ SUPS queries in $O(\log n\log\log n + k\log\log n)$ time after single character substitution. Finally, as a by-product, we propose a fully-dynamic data structure for range minimum queries (RmQs) with a constraint where the width of each query range is limited to poly-logarithmic. The constrained RmQ data structure can answer such a query in constant time and support a single-element edit operation in amortized constant time.

abstract_zh: 如果 $T[i.. j]$ 是最短回文子串，使得 $T[i.. j]$ 在 $T$ 中仅出现一次，并且 $[i, j]$ 包含 $[p, q]$，则字符串 $T$ 的回文子串 $T[i.. j]$ 被称为 $T$ 中间隔 $[p, q]$ 的最短唯一回文子串 (SUPS)。 SUPS 问题是，给定长度为 $n$ 的字符串 $T$，构造一个可以计算任何给定查询间隔的所有 SUPS 的数据结构。众所周知，经过 $O(n)$ 时间预处理后，任何 SUPS 查询都可以在 $O(α)$ 时间内得到答复，其中 $α$ 是要输出的 SUPS 数量 [Inoue et al., 2018]。在本文中，我们首先证明 $α$ 最多为 $4$，并且上限很紧。我们还表明，与 SUPS 密切相关的字符串 $T$ 的最小唯一回文子串的长度总和为 $O(n)$。然后，我们提出第一个 $O(n)$ 位数据结构，可以在恒定时间内回答任何 SUPS 查询。此外，我们提出了一种解决滑动窗口 SUPS 问题的算法，该算法可以在 $O(\log\log W)$ 时间内回答任何查询，并在摊销 $O(\logσ+ \log\log W)$ 时间内更新数据结构，其中 $W$ 是窗口的大小，$σ$ 是字母表大小。此外，我们考虑了编辑后模型中的 SUPS 问题，并提出了一种有效的算法。也就是说，我们提出了一种算法，该算法使用 $O(n)$ 时间进行预处理，并在单字符替换后在 $O(\log n\log\log n + k\log\log n)$ 时间内回答任何 $k$ SUPS 查询。最后，作为副产品，我们提出了一种用于范围最小查询（RmQ）的完全动态数据结构，其约束条件是每个查询范围的宽度仅限于多对数。受约束的RmQ数据结构可以在常数时间内回答这样的查询，并支持在摊余常数时间内的单元素编辑操作。

AI.tldr: 本文研究静态和非静态字符串中计算唯一回文子串的数据结构，提出高效算法解决最短唯一回文子串（SUPS）问题。
AI.motivation: 现有SUPS查询算法的时间复杂度依赖输出结果数量α，但α的上限不明确，且动态场景下缺乏高效数据结构。
AI.method: 通过证明α的最大值为4，设计O(n)位数据结构实现常数时间查询；针对滑动窗口和单字符编辑场景，提出基于动态规划和范围最小值查询的算法。
AI.result: 首次实现O(n)位数据结构支持常数时间SUPS查询，滑动窗口场景下查询时间复杂度为O(loglogW)，单字符编辑后查询时间复杂度为O(logn loglogn +k loglogn)。
AI.conclusion: 本文为SUPS问题提供了静态和动态场景下的高效解决方案，并提出了受限范围最小值查询的全动态数据结构。
